englishDefinitions: (intransitive) To leave, to depart, to get moving.
(intransitive or transitive) To begin or commence.
(transitive) To cause someone to talk passionately without interruption.
(intransitive, of a baby) To cry or bawl loudly.
persianTranslation: (غیر گذرا یا متعدی) برای شروع یا شروع.
(ناگذر) با شور و شوق بدون وقفه صحبت کردن.
(متعدی) وادار کردن کسی که با شور و شوق بدون وقفه صحبت کند.
(ناگذر، نوزاد) با صدای بلند گریه کردن یا داد زدن.
examples: It is quite late, I'd best get going before sunset.
We'd better get this project going. If we don't get going on it soon, we won't finish in time.
